mechanism_narrative: A persistently high federal funds rate indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance, which increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors discount future earnings more heavily.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS is above its 10-year average, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, which directly impacts consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the correlation between UNRATE change and SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales indicates strong consumer demand and confidence, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns due to improved market sentiment.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or the average 5-day return in triggered periods is not statistically positive.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, while also prompting central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as real earnings decline and discount rates rise.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL is above its 10-year average, or the negative correlation with SPX returns is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and housing starts signals a broad-based contraction in both manufacturing and construction sectors, indicating a severe economic slowdown that negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between INDPRO and HOUST changes does not significantly predict negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of periods where PAYEMS growth is positive, or the Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth signifies a strengthening economy with increasing aggregate demand, which boosts corporate profits and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate exceeds 2.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the average 20-day return in triggered periods is not statistically positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve maintains high interest rates (FEDFUNDS) while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains low, it indicates an aggressive stance to cool an overheating economy, increasing the risk of a hard landing and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with strong labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction of high FEDFUNDS and low UNRATE does not significantly predict negative returns.
